ablations.description: Intervention A: canonical-first generator prompt; baseline extractor

per_doc.narrative_excerpt:
The alice likes cats.

This indicates that, in the provided fact, alice has a liking for cats.

The bob owns dog.

This states a simple ownership relationship: bob is the owner, and dog is the owned entity.
The paris is_a city.

reconstructed_axioms: the provided fact directly in the required canonical form
per_doc.narrative_excerpt:
The einstein proposed relativity.

This states the provided fact directly in the required canonical form.
extra: the provided fact directly in the required canonical form

per_doc.narrative_excerpt:
The water contains hydrogen.

This states that hydrogen is one of the components present in water.
The sun emits light.

The plants produce oxygen.

This describes the role of plants in generating oxygen, a gas essential for many living organisms.
The dogs chase cats.

The fish eat plankton.

This sentence describes a feeding relationship in which fish consume plankton.

The birds build nests.

This states that birds construct nests, typically as places associated with sheltering eggs or young.

The shakespeare wrote hamlet.

This sentence records the stated fact in its canonical form.

The tesla invented motors.

This statement presents the fact in the required canonical form.

The darwin studied finches.

This states that the subject, “darwin,” is associated with the action “studied” and the object “finches.”

The newton described gravity.

This fact presents “newton” as the subject, “described” as the predicate, and “gravity” as the object.

The kepler discovered orbits.

This states the fact in its required canonical form.

The gold conducts electricity.

This means gold is an electrically conductive material.

The steel resists corrosion.

This means steel has a resistance to corrosion, making it suitable in contexts where durability against corrosive conditions is important.

The diamond cuts glass.

This states the fact directly: diamond is capable of cutting glass.

The copper carries current.

This means copper is being described in terms of its ability to conduct electrical current.

The iron forms rust.

This states that iron undergoes a process that results in rust.

The rome built roads.

This fact presents “rome” as the subject, “built” as the predicate, and “roads” as the object.

reconstructed_axioms: the fact in the required canonical form
per_doc.narrative_excerpt:
The greece founded democracy.

This states the fact in the required canonical form. Historically, Greece—especially ancient Athens—is widely associated with the development of democracy.
extra: the fact in the required canonical form

reconstructed_axioms: exact subject, predicate, and object tokens from the input
per_doc.narrative_excerpt:
The egypt constructed pyramids.

This sentence presents the fact in its required canonical form, preserving the exact subject, predicate, and object tokens from the input.
extra: exact subject, predicate, and object tokens from the input

per_doc.narrative_excerpt:
The persia invaded greece.

This canonical sentence records the stated fact as a direct relationship: “persia” is the subject, “invaded” is the predicate, and “greece” is the object.

The vikings explored continents.

This states that the vikings traveled beyond their homelands and reached multiple continental regions through exploration.
The electrons orbit nuclei.

The enzymes catalyze reactions.

This means enzymes help reactions proceed by acting as catalysts.

The viruses infect cells.

This means that viruses enter or affect cells as part of their biological activity.

The neurons transmit signals.

This describes the basic role of neurons in communication within the nervous system.

reconstructed_axioms: exact subject, predicate, and object tokens
per_doc.narrative_excerpt:
The muscles contract fibers.

This sentence presents the provided fact in canonical form, preserving the exact subject, predicate, and object tokens.
extra: exact subject, predicate, and object tokens

per_doc.narrative_excerpt:
The teachers explain lessons.

This states that teachers provide instruction by explaining lessons.

The scientists discover particles.

This sentence identifies scientists as the actors, “discover” as the action, and particles as the object of that action.

The engineers design bridges.

This states that engineers are responsible for creating bridge designs, applying technical knowledge to structures intended to span obstacles such as roads, rivers, or v

reconstructed_axioms: farmers as the people performing the action
wheat as the crop being grown
per_doc.narrative_excerpt:
The farmers grow wheat.

This sentence identifies farmers as the people performing the action and wheat as the crop being grown.
extra: farmers as the people performing the action
wheat as the crop being grown

per_doc.narrative_excerpt:
The chefs prepare meals.

This describes chefs carrying out the task of preparing meals.

The cars emit exhaust.

This means that cars are described as producing exhaust.

The rivers reach oceans.

This states the general relationship that rivers flow toward and eventually arrive at oceans.

The students study subjects.

This states that students engage in the activity of studying, and the things they study are subjects.

The mothers love children.

This states the relationship directly: mothers are associated with loving children.

The writers create stories.

This means that writers are responsible for producing stories, shaping ideas, characters, events, and language into narrative works.

The musicians compose symphonies.

This states that musicians create or write symphonies as part of their musical work.

The architects draw plans.

This states that architects create plans as part of their work.

reconstructed_axioms: part of their work or creative process
per_doc.narrative_excerpt:
The painters mix colors.

This states that painters combine colors as part of their work or creative process.
extra: part of their work or creative process

per_doc.narrative_excerpt:
The doctors treat patients.

This states the basic relationship that doctors provide care or medical attention to patients.

The lawyers defend clients.

This statement describes a central role of lawyers: providing defense and representation for clients in legal matters.

The judges interpret laws.

This states that judges apply legal interpretation when considering laws in the context of cases or legal questions.

The hope inspires action.

This frames hope as a motivating force: it does not remain passive, but encourages movement, effort, and purposeful response.

The music expresses emotion.

This statement presents music as a medium capable of conveying feeling, mood, and affect.

The knowledge requires effort.

This emphasizes that gaining, refining, and applying knowledge is not passive; it depends on sustained attention, practice, and deliberate engagement.

The fear paralyzes movement.

This states that fear can stop or inhibit movement, presenting fear as a force that causes immobility.

ablations.description: Intervention B: baseline generator; vocab-pinned extractor

per_doc.narrative_excerpt: Alice likes cats.
Bob owns a dog.
Paris is a city.
Albert Einstein proposed relativity.
Water contains hydrogen. This identifies hydrogen as one of the constituent components of water.
The sun emits light.
Plants produce oxygen.
Dogs chase cats.
Fish eat plankton.
Birds build nests.
Shakespeare wrote *Hamlet*.
Tesla invented motors.
Darwin studied finches. This observation is a concise statement of his work involving these birds, without adding further details beyond the given fact.
Newton described gravity.
Kepler discovered orbits. This discovery is the central fact: Kepler identified that celestial bodies follow orbits, providing a foundation for understanding their motion.
Gold conducts electricity.
Steel resists corrosion.
Diamond cuts glass.
Copper carries current.
Iron forms rust.
Rome built roads. This fact reflects Rome’s role in creating constructed routes for movement and connection.
Greece is credited with founding democracy.
Egypt constructed pyramids.
Persia invaded Greece, bringing the two powers into direct conflict.
Vikings explored continents, demonstrating a tradition of long-distance travel and navigation across vast regions.
Electrons orbit nuclei.
Enzymes catalyze reactions.
Viruses infect cells. This fact describes the central relationship between viruses and living cells: infection occurs when a virus enters or affects a cell as part of its interaction with that cell.
Neurons transmit signals.
Muscles contract fibers.
Teachers explain lessons.
Scientists discover particles. This finding becomes the central fact of the report: researchers identify particles through scientific investigation.
Engineers design bridges.
Farmers grow wheat.
Chefs prepare meals.
Cars emit exhaust.
Rivers reach oceans.
Students study subjects.
Mothers love children. This love is the central fact: it connects mothers and children through care, affection, and devotion.
Writers create stories.
Musicians compose symphonies.
Architects draw plans.
Painters mix colors as part of the painting process. By combining colors, they prepare the colors they intend to use.
Doctors treat patients.
Lawyers defend clients.
Judges interpret laws.
Hope inspires action.
Music expresses emotion. Through music, emotion can be communicated, recognized, and experienced.
Knowledge requires effort. It is not gained passively or without investment; it depends on the willingness to apply attention, persistence, and work.
Fear paralyzes movement. When fear takes hold, action stops; the body and will become fixed, unable to move forward.
